indications_for_use: The THERMOCOOL SMARTTOUCH SF-5D Catheter is indicated for cardiac electrophysiology mapping and radio-frequency (RF) ablation of drug refractory recurrent symptomatic paroxysmal atrial fibrillation (PAF) in patients who have failed or are not candidates for antiarrhythmic drug therapy.
substantial_equivalence_summary: The THERMOCOOL SMARTTOUCH SF-5D Catheter has the same intended use and technological characteristics as the predicate device K192516 (THERMOCOOL SMARTTOUCH SF Catheter). The device uses the same contact force sensing technology, irrigated tip design, and RF energy delivery mechanism. The addition of a 5th diagnostic electrode does not raise new questions of safety and effectiveness.
technological_differences: Addition of a 5th ring diagnostic electrode (SF-5D vs SF)
Modified distal shaft geometry to accommodate additional electrode
Updated electrode spacing: 4-4-4 mm (vs 2-5-2 mm in predicate)

bench_tests.test: Electrical performance — contact force sensing accuracy
bench_tests.result: Contact force accuracy ±1g across 0-200g range, meets spec

bench_tests.test: RF power delivery and temperature monitoring
bench_tests.result: Power output within ±5% of set value; thermocouple accuracy ±1°C
Flow rate 2-30 mL/min, deviation <5%
Bidirectional deflection 180° ± 10°; torque transmission ratio >0.85
Pull force > 10 N without failure
All electrodes <200Ω; inter-electrode isolation >100kΩ
Pass — cytotoxicity, sensitization, genotoxicity
EO sterility assurance level 10⁻⁶

performance_testing.clinical_data: Performance data from 47-patient clinical study (SMARTTOUCH SF-5D IDE). Primary endpoint: acute procedural success defined as bidirectional block at 3-month follow-up. Results: 89.4% success rate, comparable to predicate performance.
performance_testing.software_testing: CARTO 3 system compatibility validated per IEC 62304; no new software safety classification changes.

regulatory_strategy_notes: Applicant leveraged traditional 510(k) pathway. Substantial equivalence established through split-predicate approach: intended use from K192516; technological characteristics partially from K173408 (electrode spacing precedent). No clinical study required — bench data sufficient given technological similarity.

indications_for_use: The FARAPULSE Pulsed Field Ablation (PFA) System is indicated for cardiac electrophysiology mapping and ablation of drug refractory recurrent symptomatic persistent atrial fibrillation in patients who have failed antiarrhythmic drug therapy.
substantial_equivalence_summary: The FARAPULSE PFA System has the same intended use as predicate K192516. While PFA uses a different energy modality than RF ablation, the applicant demonstrated through bench testing and clinical data that the device achieves the same intended effect (creation of cardiac lesions for AF treatment) without raising new safety questions. FDA accepted the alternative energy modality as not raising new questions per the de novo precedent established in DEN200054.
technological_differences: Pulsed field energy delivery (irreversible electroporation) vs. RF thermal energy
Pentaspline basket catheter geometry vs. single-tip catheter
Non-thermal ablation mechanism — tissue selectivity for cardiomyocytes
Custom pulse generator (FARAWAVE Generator) vs. standard RF generator
No irrigation required — eliminates fluid management consideration

bench_tests.result: Pulse energy within ±3% of target; waveform shape within specification
All 10 electrodes achieve adequate tissue contact in cardiac anatomical models

bench_tests.test: Lesion characterization — ex vivo porcine hearts
bench_tests.result: Transmural lesions confirmed in >95% of sites; no unintended esophageal injury in 3D model
Leakage current <10µA; dielectric withstand 5kV
1000 deployment cycles without structural failure
Pass — all endpoints including hemolysis and complement activation
No interference with ICDs or pacemakers at clinical operating distances
EO sterilization validated; SAL 10⁻⁶

performance_testing.clinical_data: IMPULSE, PEFCAT, and MANIFEST-PF studies: n=473 patients, 12-month freedom from arrhythmia 78.5%. Phrenic nerve injury rate 0.6%, significantly lower than thermal ablation historical rates (~3%). No esophageal injury events.
performance_testing.software_testing: FARAWAVE Generator software classified as Class B per IEC 62304. Full SDLC documentation provided. Cybersecurity per FDA guidance (2018).

key_claims: Cardiomyocyte tissue selectivity — spares esophagus, phrenic nerve
Pentaspline basket: 5 splines × 2 electrodes = 10 contact points
Single energy application per pulmonary vein (vs. point-by-point RF)
Procedure time reduction vs. RF: ~40 min average
Compatible with major electroanatomic mapping systems
regulatory_strategy_notes: Complex 510(k) — alternative energy modality required extensive safety justification. FDA requested additional information (AI) twice during review. Key regulatory strategy: leveraged DEN200054 de novo as predicate for PFA technology class; RF ablation predicate for intended use. Clinical data from three studies submitted voluntarily to support safety profile of new energy modality.

indications_for_use: The THERMOCOOL SMARTTOUCH SF Catheter is indicated for cardiac electrophysiology mapping and RF ablation of drug refractory recurrent symptomatic paroxysmal and persistent atrial fibrillation.
substantial_equivalence_summary: Same intended use as predicate K143784. The surround flow (SF) irrigation pattern and contact force sensing are present in the predicate; this submission adds expanded AF indication (persistent, in addition to paroxysmal). Clinical data demonstrates safety and effectiveness for the expanded indication.
technological_differences: Expanded indication: persistent AF added to existing PAF indication
Surround flow irrigation pattern (unchanged from predicate)
Contact force range extended: 0–200g (vs. 0–150g in predicate)

bench_tests.result: ±1g across extended 0–200g range
Flow uniformity coefficient >0.92 across all irrigation ports
Lesion depth 4.2 ± 0.8mm at 30W/30s; no steam pop at <15mL/min irrigation
All parameters within specification
No structural failure; deflection repeatability ±5°

performance_testing.clinical_data: PRECEPT IDE study: n=333 persistent AF patients. 12-month freedom from atrial arrhythmia: 55.1% (single procedure). Serious adverse events: cardiac tamponade 1.5%, thromboembolic events 0.6%.
performance_testing.software_testing: No new software submitted. CARTO 3 compatibility confirmed through regression testing.

key_claims: Dual PAF and persistent AF indication
regulatory_strategy_notes: Traditional 510(k). Indication expansion required clinical IDE study (PRECEPT). Predicate split: technological characteristics from K143784; indication precedent built from prior persistent AF clearances. Review time extended due to clinical data review — 145 days total.

indications_for_use: The RHYTHMIA HDx Mapping System is intended for use with compatible EP catheters to record, display, and analyze intracardiac electrograms and to create electroanatomical maps of the heart to aid in the diagnosis and treatment of cardiac arrhythmias.
substantial_equivalence_summary: The RHYTHMIA HDx has the same intended use as predicate K171924 (RHYTHMIA Mapping System). Technological differences — increased channel count (128 vs 64 simultaneous channels), improved map resolution algorithm (HDx), and updated hardware platform — do not raise new safety questions. Performance testing demonstrates equivalent or superior mapping accuracy.
technological_differences: 128 simultaneous recording channels (vs. 64 in predicate)
HDx algorithm: automatic annotation and noise rejection upgrade
Updated workstation hardware — processor speed 4x improvement
New ORION mini catheter compatibility (8F vs 9F predicate)
Real-time respiratory gating — reduces map position error

bench_tests.result: Position accuracy <0.5mm RMS in 3D cardiac phantom; superior to predicate (0.8mm RMS)
SNR >40dB across all channels; crosstalk <-60dB between adjacent channels
Automatic annotation agreement with expert manual: 94.2%; false positive rate <3%
No functional degradation in standard EP lab environment
Map update latency <200ms; electrogram display latency <50ms
Penetration testing completed; no critical vulnerabilities; data encryption AES-256

performance_testing.clinical_data: No clinical study required. Mapping system performance established by bench testing per FDA-accepted methodology for electroanatomical mapping systems.
performance_testing.software_testing: Software classified IEC 62304 Class C (patient safety risk). Full SDLC documentation including SRS, SDS, unit testing, integration testing, system testing. 847 requirements traced. Regression testing 100% pass rate.

key_claims: Compatible with ORION, INTELLAMAP ORION catheters
regulatory_strategy_notes: Traditional 510(k). Software-heavy submission — IEC 62304 Class C required full SDLC documentation package. Cybersecurity review added 3 weeks to review timeline per FDA AI request. Mapping accuracy methodology pre-agreed with FDA in Q-submission (Q190982) prior to submission — accelerated review significantly.

indications_for_use: The ADVISOR HD Grid Mapping Catheter SE is intended for electrophysiological mapping of the heart. It is used to record electrograms and to pace the heart for diagnostic purposes.
substantial_equivalence_summary: Same intended use as predicate K152341 (diagnostic mapping catheter). The grid electrode configuration (4×4 array vs. linear array in predicate) represents a technological difference that does not raise new safety questions, as grid electrode arrays have established clinical precedent and the electrical safety profile is equivalent.
technological_differences: 4×4 grid electrode array (16 electrodes) vs. linear array (10 electrodes) in predicate
Omnipolar technology: simultaneous omnidirectional electrogram acquisition
Flexible grid structure — conforms to cardiac anatomy
Electrode spacing: 3mm center-to-center in grid configuration

bench_tests.result: Omnipolar signal amplitude within 10% of unipolar reference across 360° rotation
No fracture or electrode separation after 500 deflection cycles
Inter-electrode impedance >100kΩ; no shorting observed
Pass all endpoints
All 16 electrodes visible under standard fluoroscopy settings

performance_testing.clinical_data: No clinical study submitted. Bench performance data sufficient per FDA guidance for diagnostic mapping catheters.
performance_testing.software_testing: EnSite Precision system compatibility validated. No new software submission required — existing cleared software used without modification.

key_claims: Compatible with EnSite Precision Cardiac Mapping System
regulatory_strategy_notes: Traditional 510(k). Novel grid geometry justified through split predicate — electrode array from K152341, omnipolar signal processing from K171924. FDA did not request clinical data — diagnostic catheters typically clear on bench data alone. Fast review: 141 days.
